name: chronic-illness
description: Living-with-chronic-illness narrative. Probes adjacent codes (medical mistrust vs being dismissed; social isolation vs identity change) and understated lines (a quiet adaptation strategy, an aside about a friendship lost).
transcriptContent: Some mornings the pain is so bad I can't get out of bed, and I never know which day it'll be. For years the doctors told me it was all in my head, so now I second-guess whether anything they say is true. I've learned to pace myself, doing the important things early before the fatigue hits. A few friends just drifted away when I kept cancelling. I'm not the same person I was before the diagnosis, and I'm still working out who I am now.

the doctors told me it was all in my head, so now I second-guess whether anything they say is true
